text: The E0505 error follows this call chain through the MIR borrow checker: (1) `compiler/rustc_borrowck/src/lib.rs` contains `access_place()` (line 1179) which calls `check_access_for_conflict()`. When a `WriteKind::Move` conflict with an active borrow is detected, it calls `report_move_out_while_borrowed()` (line 1362). (2) `compiler/rustc_borrowck/src/borrowck_errors.rs` defines `cannot_move_when_borrowed()` (line 16) on `MirBorrowckCtxt`, which constructs the `MoveBorrow` diagnostic struct. (3) `compiler/rustc_borrowck/src/diagnostics/conflict_errors.rs` contains `report_move_out_while_borrowed()` (line 1533), which assembles the full diagnostic including place descriptions, borrow explanations, and clone suggestions, calling `cannot_move_when_borrowed()` internally (line 1552). (4) `compiler/rustc_borrowck/src/session_diagnostics.rs` defines the `MoveBorrow` struct (line 286) annotated with `#[diag(borrowck_cannot_move_when_borrowed, code = E0505)]`, which is the declarative diagnostic definition for this error. (5) `compiler/rustc_error_codes/src/error_codes/E0505.md` provides the long-form explanation for E0505 shown via `--explain`.
